summary: Phase 2 backend regression complete. 45 new Phase 2 tests + 33 Phase 1 regression tests = 78/78 passing. Covered: app_settings public (seeded defaults verified: default_gst_percent=18, default_validity_days=365, activity_sessions_required=4, commission_l1_percent=10), program_categories seeded (4 defaults) + admin CRUD + duplicate slug 409 + user list, programs full CRUD (dup-slug 409, invalid category 400, search/filter/sort, activate/deactivate/soft-delete + 404 after delete), program_modules (FK, unique (program_id, module_number)), assessments (correct_index range 400, duplicate module 409, submit computes marks+passed, wrong answer count 400, results/me), purchases (FK user+program, unique invoice 409, /me filter), progress upsert idempotency + persistence (single row), certificates (unique cert_number 409, revoke via PUT, /me only issued), referral_tree (auto-insert on register with computed level, /me downline with owner_name, /me/upline chain, admin filter by sponsor/level), bank_details (IFSC regex 422, verification resets to pending on change, admin verify, masked account list), settings (user upsert, app admin upsert, system 401/403/200), notifications (personal + broadcast, unread-count, mark-read, admin is_broadcast filter), profiles (upsert on PUT, GET default shape), activity_log (admin paginated + user /me scoped), cross-role guards (user→/admin=403, admin→/me=403), pagination metadata, and Phase 1 regression (validate-referral, admin login).

critical.issue: Seeded default categories (foundation/subscription/advanced/special) are inserted via $setOnInsert without a uuid `id` field. As a result, `GET /api/categories` returns them but they cannot be used as `category_id` when creating a program (invalid FK) nor fetched via `GET /api/categories/{id}`. All Phase 2 program creation would fail against seed data unless admin creates their own category first. Fix: add `id: str(uuid.uuid4())` inside the $setOnInsert dict in seed_program_categories() so seed rows carry a stable UUID id (idempotent). Same seed also does not set `created_by`/`updated_by` (minor).

minor.issue: test_credentials.md documents them under `/api/settings/system` and code exposes `PUT /settings/system` (single upsert) — but there is no GET-single-key or list-by-search helper for system config beyond the paginated GET. Consistent with app_settings pattern; consider adding GET /system/{key} for parity.
Doc/tests spec referred to '/verify' as PUT — code implements it as POST. Both are semantically fine but codified doc mentions PUT. Consider aligning or documenting.
Returns dict-of-key:value (not the full seeded doc structure). Cannot inspect descriptions publicly. Not blocking — deliberate for PWA bootstrap.
Returns the raw doc including the *full* account_number to the owning user (masking is only on admin list). Acceptable per code comment but worth flagging as PII surface.

GET /api/referral-tree/admin?status=... filters by literal 'status' field on tree row — but tree rows only get `status='active'` at register. Suspending is possible via PUT admin, but no test coverage in current suite. Non-blocking.

minor.endpoint: profiles.py — PUT /me on user already having a seeded row from register
minor.issue: Registration inserts a profile row with created_at/updated_at. The subsequent PUT /me uses find_one_and_update with $setOnInsert on user_membership_id — but since a row already exists, $setOnInsert is skipped (correct behaviour). No bug, just confirming.

action_items: Fix seed_program_categories in /app/backend/app/db/mongo.py to include `id: str(uuid.uuid4())` in $setOnInsert so seeded categories carry a UUID id (blocks Phase 2 program creation against seeded categories). Same for seed_app_settings for consistency (not blocking today because app_settings are keyed by `key`).
Optional: add GET /api/settings/system/{key} for parity with app_settings.
Optional: align bank-details verify method (POST vs PUT) with documentation.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_program_categories inserts default categories without generating a UUID `id` field — makes them unusable as FK targets. Fix: `"$setOnInsert": {"id": str(uuid.uuid4()), **c, ...}` (import uuid at top).

seed_admin/seed_program_categories/seed_app_settings/seed_referral_tree_root all use raw update_one/insert_one instead of going through BaseRepository, leading to missing common fields (id, created_by/updated_by). Consider a small `_seed_upsert(collection, key_field, doc)` helper that adds these consistently.
referral_tree admin PUT uses `__import__('datetime').datetime.now(...)` inline — awkward. Just add the standard `from datetime import datetime, timezone` at top of the file and call directly.
progress upsert helper uses `__import__('uuid').uuid4().hex` inline; prefer top-level `import uuid`.
app/models/phase2.py imports `date` from datetime but never uses it.

Cross-role check depends on `role` claim in JWT; if a future refresh preserves a compromised token, no server-side deny-list exists for access tokens (only refresh tokens are revocable). Consider a version/nonce claim if needed.

BaseRepository.list_paginated regex uses raw `search` string — if a user submits a regex-metacharacter payload (e.g. `.*` or backtracking pattern) it goes straight to Mongo. Consider re.escape() before wrapping in $regex to prevent ReDoS and unintended matches.

notifications.mark-read updates the shared `is_read`/`read_at` fields even for broadcast rows — meaning one user marking a broadcast as read flips it to read for everyone. For a per-user read state, a separate notification_reads collection keyed by (user_membership_id, notification_id) is needed. Acceptable for phase 2 (schema-only), but flag for phase 3.

Certificates route (/me) filters by status='issued' — good — but /me/{certificate_id} uses get_by({id, user_membership_id}) without status filter, so a revoked cert would still be visible on direct GET. Minor.
Bank details PUT resets verification_status=pending on ANY PUT including unchanged payloads — could be surprising UX. Consider comparing new vs stored fields before resetting.
activity_log `write_activity` helper is exposed but not actually called from any route (no writes wired). Route reads are functional but list will be empty until callers are added in later phases.
server.py CORS still uses `allow_credentials=True` with wildcard `*` origins (from Phase 1). Same finding as Phase 1 report.
test_credentials: Admin 9999999999 / Admin@12345. Dev OTP 123456. Company referral RW000000. Fresh 10-digit mobiles (6-9 prefix) generated per test.
seed_data_creation: Test data prefixed with 'TEST_'. All records created via API with random slugs/invoices/certificate numbers/mobiles. No teardown implemented — soft-deleted or orphaned test rows remain in Mongo but do not interfere with future runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full regression at /app/backend/tests/ — run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Phase 2 file is /app/backend/tests/test_phase2.py. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env (public URL). Note the seeded-categories-missing-id bug — if main agent hasn't fixed it, all seed-referenced Phase 2 tests still pass because the test fixture creates its own category. After the fix, add a test asserting `foundation` category has `id` in GET /categories.
rca of the issue: Seed function `seed_program_categories` in /app/backend/app/db/mongo.py inserts default category docs via update_one($setOnInsert) without generating a `id: str(uuid.uuid4())` field. The BaseRepository.create() adds `id` automatically for admin-created rows but the seed path bypasses it. Consequence: `GET /api/categories/{id}` on foundation/subscription/advanced/special returns 404, and users cannot reference them as `category_id` when creating a Program (which enforces the FK on `id`). Reproduction: `curl $API/categories -H "Authorization: Bearer <user_token>"` — items lack `id`. Mitigation: add `"id": str(uuid.uuid4())` inside the `$setOnInsert` dict in `seed_program_categories()` (and optionally seed_app_settings for consistency). Same rows will need a one-time backfill script (e.g. `db.program_categories.updateMany({id:{$exists:false}}, [{$set:{id:{$toString:{$rand:...}}}}])` or a bootstrap task) so existing deployments don't have to be wiped.
